issue_title: conda update with proxy repeatedly prompts for username and password without success
issue_body:
If I am accessing http/s through a proxy and attempt to update I start a cycle of username/password prompts:

```
$ conda update conda
Fetching package metadata: 
https proxy username: <username>

https proxy username: Password:
https proxy username: Password:
https proxy username: Password:
```

Here is what my ~/.condarc looks like:

```
channels:
 - defaults

 proxy_servers:
  http: <proxy>
  https: <proxy>
```

If I put my username and password directly into the .condarc file then `conda update conda` works though I would prefer to not to do this as I am on a shared filed system. If you need any more information I am very happy to supply this. Thanks!

issue_title: Getting index for platforms other than the one you're on
issue_body:
There used to be a way to do this: https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/4.1.x/conda/api.py#L21-L23

but that seems to be gone in 4.2: https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/4.2.x/conda/api.py#L10 - the platform argument is still there, but it does not do anything.

Is there an equivalent way with the new config?  I looked for both of the two functions (```normalize_urls``` and ```get_channel_urls```) but neither of them seem to exist.  Where are you appending the platform to the URL, and can we make the platform changeable?

This is essential for the new build system, which examines dependencies and computes the necessary builds for all platforms from one central platform, so I'm marking it as blocking.

issue_title: BAM file SQ header not parsing correctly
issue_body:
Using the following code:

```python
from __future__ import print_function
import pysam

filename = "ftp://ftp.1000genomes.ebi.ac.uk/vol1/ftp/phase3/data/HG00533/alignment/HG00533.mapped.ILLUMINA.bwa.CHS.low_coverage.20120522.bam"

samFile = pysam.AlignmentFile(filename)
for referenceInfo in samFile.header['SQ']:
    print()
    for k, v in referenceInfo.items():
        print(k, v)
```
I get
```
LN 35477943
M5 5b6a4b3a81a2d3c134b7d14bf6ad39f1
SN hs37d5
UR ftp://ftp.1000genomes.ebi.ac.uk/vol1/ftp/technical/reference/phase2_reference_assembly_sequence/hs37d5.fa.gz        AS:NCBI37       SP:Human
```
Note that AS and SP are not being correctly parsed out here, but are included in the UR line. I think these should be included as keys in the info dict.

Hi,

I ran into what looks like a bug related to iterators in Pysam. Calling ``fetch`` appears to throw off original ``Samfile`` iterator. Example code:

```
File Edit Options Buffers Tools Python Outline Help                                                                                
import pysam

# test 1: correct result                                                                                                           
bam = pysam.Samfile("./pysam.bam", "rb")
other_reads = bam.fetch(reference="chrRibo", start=None, end=None)
n = 0
for r in bam:
    n += 1
print "First: Got %d reads" %(n)

# test 2: incorrect result                                                                                                         
bam = pysam.Samfile("./accepted_hits.bam", "rb")
other_reads = bam.fetch(reference="chrRibo", start=None, end=None)
# Now we iterate through fetched reads                                                                                             
for x in other_reads:
    pass
n = 0
for r in bam:
    n += 1
print "Second time: Got %d reads" %(n)
```

Example file is:

BAM: http://genes.mit.edu/burgelab/yarden/pysam.bam
BAM index: http://genes.mit.edu/burgelab/yarden/pysam.bam.bai

This gives:

```
$ python pysam.py
First: Got 2112633 reads
Second time: Got 62089 reads
```

It seems like iterating through the results of a separate fetch call shouldn't affect original iterator. Even if it does, not sure how to interpret the numbers of reads that are left in the file. 

Is this a bug? if so, how can this be fixed? 

I am using pysam '0.8.1'.

**Update**: I believe setting ``multiple_iterators=True`` fixes this, but I found it quite counterintuitive. Maybe it would be easier to have ``multiple_iterators=True`` by default? 

Thanks!

issue_title: Code folding issues with inline PERFORM
issue_body:
The following issues can be seen in the attached image:
![marked_text](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/6699539/14559536/cb1a7f38-0309-11e6-8388-f24066e0e610.PNG)

* inline ``PERFORM`` cannot be collapsed
* ``END-PERFORM`` is sometimes used as end marker for ``IF``

It's likely not that easy to recognize a ``PERFORM`` as inline as the simplest rule is "if PERFORM is followed by  a procedure name it is an out-of-line PERFORM" and pycode doesn't know anything about procedure names.

Even taken into account that pycode knows about "tags" (procedures, identifiers [including ``FUNCTION``s]) doesn't help for recognizing an out-of-line-perform (which was my first thought). ``PERFORM tag`` can either be a out-of-line perform:

~~~~cobol
       PERFORM sec
       PERFORM sec
        VARYING a FROM 1 BY 1
        UNTIL a = 5
       PERFORM sec a TIMES
       PERFORM par1
       PERFORM par1
        THRU par2
~~~~

or the start of an inline perform:

~~~~cobol
       PERFORM a TIMES
            WRITE stuff(a)
       END-PERFORM
~~~~

Am I wrong and pycode already knows if a tag is a procedure or identifier?

## Steps to reproduce

I opened an 18MB file.  I'm not sure if the content of the file was the issue or the size.  This is my first day of using COBOL so I'm afraid I can't be more help...

## Traceback

```
  File "D:\Documents\OpenCobolIDE\open_cobol_ide\controllers\file.py", line 81, in request_open
  File "D:\Documents\OpenCobolIDE\open_cobol_ide\controllers\file.py", line 90, in open_file
  File "D:\Documents\OpenCobolIDE\open_cobol_ide\controllers\edit.py", line 155, in add_editor
  File "D:\Documents\OpenCobolIDE\open_cobol_ide\view\editors\__init__.py", line 15, in update_editor_settings

AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'color_scheme'
```

## System information


- OpenCobolIDE: 4.6.6
- GnuCOBOL: GNU Cobol 1.1.0
- Python: 3.4.3 (32bits)
- Qt: 5.5.0
- PyQt: 5.5
- pyqode.core: 2.6.9
- pyqode.cobol: 2.6.4
- pyqode.qt: 2.7.dev0
- pygments: 2.0.2
- QDarkStyle: 2.1

Currently only members are stored which are all contollers and responders.  We need a way to identify them.

The xml looks like:
```
<group flag="132">
<address>30785</address>
<name>Family Room Table</name>
<parent type="3">52063</parent>
<deviceGroup>23</deviceGroup>
<ELK_ID>C02</ELK_ID>
<members>
<link type="16">21 8F 5B 1</link>
<link type="16">1D FF E9 5</link>
<link type="16">1C 46 CB 1</link>
<link type="32">33 1 49 6</link>
<link type="32">2A E 4D 8</link>
</members>
</group>
```

Where 32 is a responder and 16 is a controller.

Ryan requested:

I would prefer to have the members array only be responders and then a new array called controllers be created. Good catch with that. Looking at that type number, I'd bet dollars to donuts that there is also an 8, 4, 2, and 1. They probably then treat those as 6 bit binary flags. So:
100000 - Responder (32)
010000 - Controller (16)
001000 - Something (8)
000100 - Something (4)
000010 - Something (2)
000001 - Something (1)
000000 - ISY System Level Pairing, Not a true Insteon pairing (this is my best guess)

My guess is that they used this design so that nodes could belong to multiple categories. For us, however, I don't think we need to worry about this right now. Let's just make sure that 0, 16, and 32 are handled. I think 0 should be treated like 32 for now.

issue_title: Update MathJax to 2.6, to fix rendering artifact
issue_body:
I'm getting the following artifact of a right sided border on rendered MathJax objects (see image) from either manually created LaTeX code (as below) or Sympy expression renderings. The issue happens for both $ ... $ and $$ ... $$ items, where the MathJax object terminates; inline expressions will have them, too. This was working on the default install of a plain Python installation, using notebook 4.1, but under the most recent distribution of Anaconda, it's happening.

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/197778/12616595/0625ccb8-c4da-11e5-9c58-3f1e90dabf36.png)

```
We know the following:
* Side AC = 500
* Side BC = 300
* Angle BAC = 36.87
* Angle ABC = 90

We need to solve for side AB. As described, angle BAC allows us to use its cosine ratio to derive AB, since it is adjacent and side AC is opposite angle ABC, and thus is the hypotenuse:

$\cos{36.87} = \frac{AB}{500}$  
$500 \cdot \cos{36.87} = AB$  
$400 = AB$

We can check this using the Pythagorean theorem, $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$:  
$300^2+b^2=500^2$  
$90000+b^2=250000$  
$b^2 = 160000$  
$b = \sqrt{160000} = 400$
```

I can do further testing, but wanted to at least bring this to your attention first to know if I'm missing something here. Thanks!

issue_title: Error when using vstack / hstack vs. concatenate
issue_body:
Hi guys,
I am seeing errors when using vstack / hstack vs. using concatenate to assemble matrices. The below code block reproduces this.

Thanks once again for the awesome library :+1:

```
from autograd import numpy as np
import autograd

def vstack_test(matrix):
    stacked = np.vstack([matrix, matrix + 1])
    stacked = np.hstack([stacked, stacked + 1])
    dummy_left = np.ones(stacked.shape[0])
    dummy_right = np.ones(stacked.shape[1])
    
    stacked_inv = np.linalg.inv(stacked)
    return np.dot(np.dot(dummy_left, stacked_inv), dummy_right)

def concat_test(matrix):
    stacked = np.concatenate([matrix, matrix + 1])
    stacked = np.concatenate([stacked, stacked + 1], axis=1)
    dummy_left = np.ones(stacked.shape[0])
    dummy_right = np.ones(stacked.shape[1])
    
    stacked_inv = np.linalg.inv(stacked)
    return np.dot(np.dot(dummy_left, stacked_inv), dummy_right)

mat = np.random.normal(size=(2,2))
print vstack_test(mat)
print concat_test(mat)
print autograd.grad(concat_test)(mat)
 # next line throws an error, but the function should be equivalent to the previous line
print autograd.grad(vstack_test)(mat)
```

- [ ] Document it. It is the same procedure as planemo but it isn't documented - https://github.com/galaxyproject/planemo/blob/master/docs/developing.rst.
- [ ] Add others' to PyPI roles.

* it's 1000 lines of code
* It contains multiple functions that are over 50 lines long.
* It can contains a class (that should be able to simplify a few things) but in this class multiple methods are over 50 lines long.
* A global log variable is defined
* It overwrites the ConnectionError that is in standard library.
* It has by far the highest complexity of all files in ephemeris (18)

Shed-tools is not pleasant to work at. Navigating through it is a jungle. It should be totally refactored.
I have a few suggestions how to approach this:
* Take `get_tool_list_from_galaxy.py` as an example. This was done for `run-data-managers` as well, and as a result complexity was dropped, while at the same time increasing functionality.
Essentially this means shed_tools should be written as a class that:
    - Has a method to output currently installed tools
    - Has a method to check whether a tool is currently installed
    - Has a method to install a tool
    - Has a method to test a tool (Thanks a lot @jmchilton !)
    - Has some sort of install_tools method for multiple tools including logging.
* The parsing of the tool list yaml may be moved to a separate file
* Some stuff from get_tool_list from galaxy might be reused in shed-tools. (Like determining current tool list)
* Some redundant command line options should be removed. Installing by passing a yaml string? OK. Installing by tool_list file? OK. Installing by specifying every frigging tool attribute on the commandline? We can drop that IMO.

This is something I have been thinking about doing it for a while. Just putting it out there before we increase the complexity of shed-tools even further. I think refactoring will also improve the ability to test using pytest #80.
The build engine should be cached. In fact, scikit-build should not call `cmake` but rather `cmake --build .` if cmake has already been run.

```py3tb
    for (line_number, offset, text, check) in runner:

    for error in self.visit_tree(child):

```

It is interesting because before I started whittling down the flake8 errors (all I was doing was adding documentation really), `pep8-naming` was doing OK.  Then out of the blue this crash!

I found a similar issue [which was resolved by checking if `id` was available](https://github.com/JBKahn/flake8-print/pull/8/files).

Before I start working on a PR that changes any `xxx.id` to be `getattr(xxx, "id", None)`, since there are a lot more uses of `.id` in `pep8-naming`, anything special to look out for?

The file being checked here is actually deliberately breaking `pep8-naming` conventions, but I was working on the other flake8 errors before I `#noqa` the classes that intentionally break things.  I wonder if that is somehow related (the fact that there are actual errors causing this issue)?

issue_title: Question: How the fixture can communicate that it is terminating so that the master can re-schedule the tests to other workers
issue_body:
I tried sys.exit() in a session scope fixture but even then the test is marked as Error. Also the same worker was allocated with more tests and they also end up in error. What I am trying to achieve is if a worker terminates then the master should re-schedule to tests to other workers.

Contests of conftest.py:
```python
import pytest
import sys
import os

sys.stdout = sys.stderr

def pytest_cmdline_preparse(args):
    worker = os.environ.get('PYTEST_XDIST_WORKER')
    if 'xdist' in sys.modules and not worker:  # pytest-xdist plugin
        num = 2
        args[:] = ["-sv", "-n", str(num)] + args

def read_device_list():
    return ["192.168.72.11", "192.168.72.12"]

def pytest_configure(config):
    # read device list if we are on the master
    if not hasattr(config, "slaveinput"):
        config.iplist = read_device_list()

def pytest_configure_node(node):
    # the master for each node fills slaveinput dictionary
    # which pytest-xdist will transfer to the subprocess
    node.slaveinput["ipadr"] = node.config.iplist.pop()

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def device(request):
    slaveinput = getattr(request.config, "slaveinput", None)
    if slaveinput is None: # single-process execution
        ipadr = read_device_list()[0]
    else: # running in a subprocess here
        ipadr = slaveinput["ipadr"]
    if ipadr == "192.168.72.11":
        print("192.168.72.11 Crashed")
        sys.exit(-11)
    return Device(ipadr)

class Device:

    def __repr__(self):
```

issue_title: Is there a race condition when allocating stock?
issue_body:
there are several methods in the `partner.StockRecord` model that look a bit like this:

```
def allocate(self, amount, reference='', status=''):
        """
        Convenience method for ring-fencing money against this source
        """
        self.amount_allocated += amount
        self.save()
```

I didn't dig too deep but I didn't find any use of `select_for_update` in the source so I think even knowing that everything's wrapped in a per-request transaction there's a possible race condition here?

Should the methods do something like this to be safer?

```
def allocate(self, amount, reference='', status=''):
        """
        Convenience method for ring-fencing money against this source
        """
        self.__class__.objects.select_for_update().filter(pk=self.pk).update(
            amount_allocated=F('amount_allocated') + amount)
```

issue_title: Download selected orders as CSV is broken
issue_body:
When the Download button is used to download the selected orders as CSV at orders dashboard, the resulting CSV file doesn't contain any data, instead has the keys in place of values. CSV file downloaded for one order in the sandbox site has the below content,

Order number,Order value,Date of purchase,Number of items,Order status,Customer email address,Deliver to name,Bill to name
number,value,date,num_items,status,customer,shipping_address_name,billing_address_name

How would one go about logically adding adding a permissions set for Customer Support staff who should have limited dashboard access to all customers and all orders? (Think phone support staff in a call center.) 

I've been researching the `partner.dashboard_access` permission but it doesn't quite fit with our scenario given the support staff work in a call center and not for the fulfillment partner. 

I'm asking for guidance on where the best place this would be done or how to go about achieving a permissions configurable dashboard navigation for different user roles. 

We would like to add dashboard access for:
- search customers
- view/edit customers
- view/edit orders (edit as in take action on the order for phone support)

Hi there,
I'm running letsencrypt on Ubuntu 14.04.3 behind an installation of haproxy.
I've configured haproxy to forward every request on port 443 containing `acme-challenge` to the letsencrypt standalone server running on port 444 which I changed with `--dvsni-port 444`.

I built a script that automatically refreshes all of my certificates **which worked flawlessly** for my configuration until now.
Obviously the parameter `--dvsni-port` is no longer available, so I changed the call to use `--tls-sni-01-port 444`.

However, this does not seem to work:
`/root/.local/share/letsencrypt/bin/letsencrypt certonly --email admin@***** --domains api.*****.com --standalone --standalone-supported-challenges tls-sni-01 --tls-sni-01-port 444 --debug`

issue_body:
I´m using `FeatureGroup` and `LayerControl` to set a bunch of layers. When the map is drawn all layers are visible. Is there a way to show only one layer on rendering and keep `overlay == True`?

It would be awesome if you could insert a CSS file when building this to style the page a bit. Or add additional elements to the webpage itself.

This is my workaround for now:

```python
# inset css file post map creation and title it.
            soup = BeautifulSoup(open("map/{}/index.html".format(year)))
            meta = soup.find('meta')
            css = soup.new_tag('link')

            body = soup.find('body')

```

Tough call - on the one hand it is good to be accommodating but on the other hand we should trust that people are adhering to what appears to be a pretty definitive interface definition:

Here is the script used to load image data into data frame:

```
import turicreate as tc

# load data
image_data = tc.image_analysis.load_images('images', with_path=True)  
  
labels = ['1', '4', '8', '9', 'a']
  
def get_label(path, labels=labels):  
    for label in labels:  
        if label in path:  
            return label  
  
image_data['label'] = image_data['path'].apply(get_label)  
 
# save data  
image_data.save('chars.sframe')
```

```
import turicreate as tc
```

I am using pyenv as my version manager. When executing awsume it will print out 
```
$ awsume -s my-profile
User profile credentials will expire: 2018-02-02 22:25:56
Role profile credentials will expire: 2018-02-02 20:12:17+01:00
export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=ACCESSKEY
export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=KEYID
export AWS_SESSION_TOKEN=SOMESESSIONTOKEN
export AWS_SECURITY_TOKEN=SOMETOKEN
export AWS_REGION=eu-central-1
export AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=eu-central-1
export AWSUME_PROFILE=my-profile
```

However, when I call `awsume my-profile` and then try to print out one of the variables, they are empty.
Using `$(awsume -s my-profile)` instead makes it work.
 I suppose this has something to do with the way pyenv handles executables. The relevant line is

`exec "/home/me/.pyenv/libexec/pyenv" exec "$program" "$@"
`

which as far as I understand starts a subshell and prevents the variables from being exported.

Is there a way to make awsume work with pyenv or am I doing something wrong?

issue_title: Find the right IAM and ACM Certificate on senza create
issue_body:
senza create use currently a _stupid_ way to select the IAM certificate. They replace all points (.) in the domain with minus (-) and try to use this.

Better: Read the issued domain names form all IAM and ACM certificate and match with with most matched and newest certificate.

Just wanted to clarify some things since I successfully built and installed on Windows 10 and this wasn't mentioned in the README.

Even though the build instruction says use `python scripts/mk_make.py -x --python`, this can build a package that works in python3.

The `z3.exe` appears to be independent and can work anywhere, you can copy the built version wherever. The `libz3.dll` needs to be discoverable on the PATH. So you can copy both z3.exe and libz3.dll into somewhere on the PATH.

Then the `build/python/z3` directory should be copied into the site-packages directory of Python. On Windows, this isn't done automatically, instead you have to find your Windows site-packages: like `python3 -c 'import site; print(site.getsitepackages())'`, then copy the entire directory there.

Then to test if it finally all works run:

```
from z3 import *
Int('x')
```

issue_title: Solver parameter "timeout" doesn't behave correctly when setting timeout of zero
issue_body:
The documentation for the solver parameter "timeout" says

```
timeout (unsigned int) timeout (in milliseconds) (0 means no timeout) (default: 0)
```

However if I try and set a timeout of zero when I try to use the solver it returns ``Z3_L_UNDEF`` with the reason ( given by ``Z3_solver_get_reason_unknown()``) ``cancelled``.

Here's a simple demonstration. In ``fpa_example()`` in ``examples/c/test_capi.c`` add the following code just after creating the solver ``s``.

```
  // Set "no timeout" for some reason this causes
  // the solver to get "canceled"... what!?
  Z3_params solverParams = Z3_mk_params(ctx);
  Z3_params_inc_ref(ctx, solverParams);
  Z3_symbol timeoutParamStrSymbol = Z3_mk_string_symbol(ctx, "timeout");
  // "timeout (unsigned int) timeout (in milliseconds) (0 means no timeout) (default: 0)"
  Z3_params_set_uint(ctx,
                     solverParams,
                     timeoutParamStrSymbol,
                     0); // no timeout
  Z3_solver_set_params(ctx, s, solverParams);
  Z3_params_dec_ref(ctx, solverParams);
```

With this code added when running the example you see

```
FPA-example
c4: (and (= x_plus_y (fp.add rm x y))
     (= x_plus_y (fp #b0 #b10000000100 #x5000000000000))
     (not (= rm roundTowardZero))
     (not (fp.isZero y))
     (not (fp.isNaN y))
     (not (fp.isInfinite y)))
Error: canceled
```

As you can see the solver fails with reason ``canceled``.

When I set a non-zero timeout the solver seems to behave correctly. Although sometimes I get ``timeout`` and sometimes I get ``unknown`` back from ``Z3_solver_get_reason_unknown()`` which is a little annoying.

Any idea what is going on here? Is this a bug or am I using the API incorrectly?

issue_title: How to query models for boxed objectives?
issue_body:
Is there a way to query z3 so I can get the satisfying models for a specific objective in case I have boxed (or pareto) objectives?

This is what I'm trying:

```
(declare-fun a () Int)

(assert (>  a  2))
(assert (<= a 10))

(minimize (+ (* 4 a) 2))
(maximize (+ 5 (* 2 a)))

(set-option :opt.priority box)

(check-sat)
(get-value (a))
```

This returns:

```
sat
(objectives
 ((+ (* 4 a) 2) 14)
 ((+ 5 (* 2 a)) 25)
)
((a 3))
```

I interpret this as the first goal `4a+2` optimized to a minimal value of `14` under the assignment `a=3`; which is nicely returned by the `(get-value (a))` call.

Similarly, `2a+5` has been maximized at value `25`, when `a=10`.

But, how do I query z3 for the `a=10` value, i.e., the assignment for the second objective?

In general, if I have `box` objectives, which goal does a `get-value` call refer to? It seems the answer is the very first goal from my experiments: Is that always true? How do I access the values for other goals? (Or better yet, is there a way to specify which objective `get-value` should refer to?)

I also have a very similar question for `pareto` optimization: How do I obtain values of model values for different objective values in that case? (But that seems to be a slightly different request I guess; so I can file a different ticket for that if necessary.)
MultiFS.listdir() should probably eliminate duplicates from its return, to give the expected semantics of listdir()?   @willmcgugan

issue_title: Add check for unicode string value in BVV
issue_body:
I was playing around and ran into some trouble while processing a unicode string at [1]. It's not too hard to handle a unicode string here. Obtaining the correct byte length for multi-byte unicode characters should still work with `len` on unicode strings. It should also return the correct hex encoded representation.

However, I haven't checked elsewhere for similar issues. Would the better solution be to create a hex string in my program before passing it in? Ex: '\xc3\xab\xcc\x81a\xc3\xba' instead of 'ë́aú'

I can submit a pull-request if you'd like the change that handles unicode.

issue_title: Correction for Django Warning for Template Loader +
issue_body:
The following warning is issued:

```
admin_tools: (admin_tools.W001) You must add "admin_tools.template_loaders.TemplateLoader" in your template loaders variable, see: https://django-admin-tools.readthedocs.org/en/latest/configuration.html
```

When added as instructed:

```
TEMPLATES = [
    {
        'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
        'DIRS': [  
		...
        ],
        'APP_DIRS': False,
        'OPTIONS': {
            'context_processors': [
		...
            ],
            'loaders': [
                'admin_tools.template_loaders.TemplateLoader',
```

the following error is returned:

```
  File "PATH/TO/venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/django/utils/module_loading.py", line 29, in import_string
    return getattr(module, class_name)
ImportError: Module "django.template.loaders.app_directories" does not define a "TemplateLoader" attribute/class
```

I noticed that ```admin_tools.template_loaders``` has a class named ```Loader``` as seen:

https://github.com/django-admin-tools/django-admin-tools/blob/master/admin_tools/template_loaders.py#L41

More than happy to issue a PR if that helps, wanted to confirm this minor issue or get corrected in the case I missed something. 

Thanks for all the hard work.

issue_title: Use long PGP ID for debian install instructions
issue_body:
On https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/blame/57e623cd7d5f3912ffae5af86e104d93577fedb0/doc/distribution_linux.md#L17 it recommends

```
sudo apt-key adv --keyserver keys.gnupg.net --recv-key 6F3EFCDE
```

short PGP keys are trivial to forge (32-bit integers), so it would be better if you could use the long PGP ID instead in the instrall instructions.

issue_title: Hub modules mobilenet_v2 and pnasnet_large have broken fine-tuning
issue_body:
The following TensorFlow Hub modules are unusable for fine-tuning, that is, for use with `hub.Module(..., tags={"train"}, trainable=True)`:

  * `google/imagenet/pnasnet_large/*/1` (both types, i,e., feature_vector
     and classification),
  * `google/imagenet/mobilenet_v2_*_*/*/1` (all feature depths, sizes
     and types).

This is fixed in version /2 of the respective modules, which will be available on https://tfhub.dev/ very soon. (For documentation, please see [docs/modules/image.md](https://github.com/tensorflow/hub/blob/master/docs/modules/image.md) until the update to tensorflow.org has rolled out.)

**Hub users who seek to fine-tune of pnasnet_large or mobilenet_v2 should switch to module version /2 immediately.** For all other image modules, there is no update, and version /1 remains current.

Uses of a module without fine-tuning, notably by [examples/image_retraining/retrain.py](https://github.com/tensorflow/hub/blob/master/examples/image_retraining/retrain.py), are unaffected. Hub users doing that can update to version /2 at their leisure, with no difference expected.

The underlying technical problem with more information for module *creators* is described in issue https://github.com/tensorflow/hub/issues/85

issue_title: Allow programmatically configuring the graph visualization
issue_body:
Migrated from https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/issues/5305.

@eamartin says:

> I'm repeatedly making small tweaks to graphs and visualizing them in TensorBoard.
> Here are two feature requests for TensorBoard that would make this workflow significantly smoother:
> 
> (1)
> Allow the user to specify that a node should always be drawn in the main graph or as an auxiliary node.  Alternatively, add an option to draw all nodes in main graph.
> 
> Currently, TensorBoard chooses to put nodes in a subgraph as auxiliary nodes (within the subgraph). Adding a node to the main graph consists of "open subgraph, click auxiliary node to add to main graph", which needs to be repeated for each auxiliary node to move because "add to main graph" collapses all subgraphs. This pain could also be partially alleviated by an option to "Shift + Click" to add/remove multiple nodes from the main graph at once.
> 
> (2)
> Allow the user to force edges to be drawn when both nodes are in the main graph.
> 
> Example case:
> Let op ``X`` be in the top level graph. Let ``SG`` be a subgraph and let ``SG`` contain 10 subgraphs ``F_i`` for ``i=1,...,10`` that all take ``X`` as input.
> TensorBoard will draw an edge from ``X`` to ``SG``, but each ``F_i`` will have it's dependence on ``X`` noted as an auxiliary input. There is no way to add ``X`` to the main graph because it is already in the main graph (but visualized as an aux input). 
> It would nice for there to be a way for there to be one edge from ``X`` to ``SG``, and then 10 edges connecting the entry point of ``X`` into ``SG`` with each of the ``F_i``. Ideally this option would also have a programmatic interface or at least a default to set.

Currently we look at REDIRECTION_CHARS in many places in the code: parsing, tab completion, etc. REDIRECTION_CHARS is a constant and is set to `['>', '|'`. When we use this constant, we have to do additional code to make sure that we catch `>>` as a valid redirection, but not `||`.

We should really convert REDIRECTION_CHARS into a constant called REDIRECTION_TOKENS, which would be set to `['>', '>>', '|']`. This would force code that uses this constant to be generalized so that if we change the constant, the code still works.

issue_title: "renew" command fails in normal use without useful error
issue_body:
Hi,

I created my certificate with something along these lines:

```bash
./certbot-auto certonly -n -d www.domain1.com -d www.domain2.com --webroot --webroot-path /home/donmccurdy/public --expand
```

Later, to renew my certificate manually, and tried this:

```bash
./certbot-auto renew
```

Output:

```
➜  ~ ./certbot-auto renew 
Requesting root privileges to run certbot...
  /home/donmccurdy/.local/share/letsencrypt/bin/letsencrypt renew
Saving debug log to /var/log/letsencrypt/letsencrypt.log
Currently, the renew verb is only capable of renewing all installed certificates that are due to
be renewed; individual domains cannot be specified with this action. If you would like to renew
specific certificates, use the certonly command. The renew verb may provide other options for
selecting certificates to renew in the future.
```

I couldn't parse that error, and the log wasn't any more specific, so for the last few months I've been manually creating a new certificate instead of renewing the existing one. I finally tracked the problem down, [thanks to this thread](https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/cert-renewal-error/11278). An entry had appeared in `cli.ini` (a file I never edited myself, that I'm aware of) hardcoding those domains, and the `renew` command apparently refers to that file.

Because domains were not specified in the command I issued, could this error message be more explicit about why it is failing? Or perhaps call some attention to the setting it inherits from `cli.ini`?

Cheers

certbot-auto certonly --force-renewal --standalone --standalone-supported-challenges http-01
But this new syntax will bomb out
certbot-auto certonly --force-renewal --standalone --preferred-challenges http-01
with "MisconfigurationError: At least one of the (possibly) required ports is already taken."  .. because it tests 443 despite being told not to.  
This is probably just a problem in the commandline parsing somewhere, or a function referring back the old config values.

At the moment, if I run "mavproxy" as my process in the container, it exits with error 1 after a few seconds.

Running the docker container interactively, it continues to run ok. Is there an option eg "--non-interactive" or could one easily be made to suit small setups running in docker?

issue_title: Assure all API errors are returned in json format
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

At the moment all error descriptions in the API are [provided in plain text](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/master/raiden/api/rest.py#L358) as part of the RPC response. That is sometimes not what a user would expect as can be seen from https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/issues/1020

## Solution

Make sure that all errors are returned as part of a json response object with key `"error"` and the value being a string of the description.


## Tasklist

- [ ] Assure all errors are returned in a json object
- [ ] Update documentation of the API to reflect the change
- [ ] Add a changelog entry.

issue_title: Raiden won't start after a channel has been settled
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Settling a contract also makes the contract commit suicide. This means that there is node code at it's address after this point. However when we start the blockchain service we check that there is code deployed at all addresses (https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/master/raiden/network/rpc/client.py#L884-L887). This means that Raiden throws an exception on startup.

Removing the `.raiden` dir does not even solve the problem.

## Solution

Either allow Raiden to start even with settled channels or remove "unregister" the channels in the client once settled.

Addressing this will make our codebase more maintainable and improve [Better Code Hub](https://bettercodehub.com)'s **Couple Architecture Components Loosely** guideline rating! 👍 

Here's the gist of this guideline:
- **Definition** 📖 
Minimize the amount of interface code; that is, code in modules that are both called from and call modules of other components ("throughput"), and code in modules that are called from modules of other components ("incoming").
- **Why**❓ 
Having loose coupling between top-level components makes it easier to maintain them in isolation.
- **How** 🔧 
You can hide a component's implementation details through various means, e.g. using the "abstract factory" design pattern.

You can find more info about this guideline in [Building Maintainable Software](http://shop.oreilly.com/product/0636920049159.do). 📖

issue_title: Deferred status transition with Lpar.activate() and deactivate()
issue_body:
When the `Lpar.activate()` and `deactivate()` methods complete (e.g. by returning after being called with `wait_for_completion=True`), the LPAR state may not have reached the desired state, and a subsequent status retrieval still reports the old state.

This does not happen always, and the exact circumstances are not fully understood at this point.

As a result, users of the zhmcclient have to code around this and loop on status retrieval until the desired state is reached.

The zhmcclient could take care of that by performing the stats polling inside of these methods for the `wait_for_completion=True` case, making life easier for the user. In the `wait_for_completion=False` case, adressing this is more complex, but not impossible, given that we have PR #182: The async method could return a new subclass of `Job` that implements its check and wait methods such that completion includes polling for the desired status.

Discussion points:
* Should we do this at all?
* Should a timeout be added? (see Issue #6)

issue_title: Logging issues with GLOG (composite logging statements don't always work).
Plasma manager being marked dead because call to Create takes 60 seconds.
issue_body:
While debugging #670, I came across the following problem.

The setup is the following.

- One head node (m4.16xlarge)
- 99 other nodes (c4.xlarge)
- Run the following workload on the head node

    ```python
    @ray.remote
    def f():
        return 1

    %time l = ray.get([f.remote() for _ in range(10000000)])
    ```

After several minutes, the plasma manager logs a fatal error for taking too long to send a heartbeat. Adding in some timing statements, the problem seems to be that the call

```c++
  Status s = conn->manager_state->plasma_conn->Create(
      object_id, data_size, NULL, metadata_size, &(buf->data));
```

in `process_data_request` in `plasma_manager.cc`, which creates an object in the object store (and which is a blocking call), takes **60 seconds** https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/96962cdee0c6803695505dfb3c3cdcc451ce7a5f/src/plasma/plasma_manager.cc#L911 (this is an absurd amount of time). Note that at this point in the script, that line has only been called about 700 times. All prior calls to that line took around 0ms, and all subsequent calls to that line took around 6ms. Note that the number 700 seems very small. This could be because the vast majority of tasks are getting executed locally.

issue_title: [V3 Cleanup] Handle getting coroutine prefixes for [p]cleanup bot
issue_body:
### Type

- Bug

### Description
Coroutine prefixes aren't implemented for `cleanup bot`. This is kind of important since Red's default prefix is a coroutine.

This is pretty easy; we can just use `await bot.get_prefix(ctx.message)`, since this actually just returns a list of server prefixes.

[Relevant Sentry issue](https://sentry.io/will-tekulve/bot-development/issues/420947439/)

```
TypeError: argument of type 'coroutine' is not iterable
  File "discord/ext/commands/core.py", line 62, in wrapped
    ret = yield from coro(*args, **kwargs)
  File "cleanup/cleanup.py", line 234, in cleanup_bot
    if '' in prefixes:

Exception in command 'cleanup bot'
```

issue_title: No error on dc.load if product requested doesn't exist
issue_body:
### Expected behaviour
If the requested product doesn't exist (i.e. typo, renamed etc), I would expect an error telling me the product doesn't exist.

### Actual behaviour
dc.load works without error, but just returns an empty xarray.

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour
While accessing the WOFS datacube. The product was renamed to `old_wofs`. I was trying to call the product by an older name. The load "worked" but loaded nothing.
Sigh. But between the weird tempfile issues and #10, it's clearly necessary.

This has been discussed before, I'm just creating an issue to keep track of it. @yadudoc has gotten a nice Jenkins server setup. This can do everything Travis can do + tests where we actually run on remote resources (because we can set up private keys on it). So for simplicity we should move the current nosetests and linting to Jenkins, so everything is done in the same place.
